The search functionality is affected - text matching doesn't work with params.","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:47:58Z"},{"id":429,"issue_id":"br-21uwu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"VioletDawn: Deep dive into frankensqlite found the root cause of remaining 14 test failures:

1. UPDATE statements report '1 row affected' but don't actually change data in the database
2. SELECT queries with parameters return rows but all string columns show the same value (the first column value)

Example from test:
- INSERT recipe with name='v1' - works
- UPDATE SET name='v2' WHERE id=1 - reports 1 row affected but doesn't actually update
- SELECT WHERE id=1 - returns row but ALL fields show 'v1' instead of proper values

This is a VDBE parameter binding bug in frankensqlite affecting both Variable opcode usage and UPDATE SET operations. Requires fix in fsqlite-vdbe or fsqlite-core.

-{"id":"br-22iss","title":"frankensqlite UPDATE statements don't persist changes (VDBE parameter bug)","description":"## Summary
UPDATE statements in frankensqlite report '1 row affected' but don't actually persist the changes to the database.

## Reproduction
From mcp-agent-mail-db search_recipes tests:
1. INSERT recipe with name='v1' - works correctly
2. UPDATE SET name='v2' WHERE id=1 - reports 1 row affected  
3. SELECT WHERE id=1 - returns row with name still 'v1'

## Root Cause Analysis
VioletDawn investigated the VDBE execution path:
- Variable opcode correctly reads parameters from bindings
- SqliteValue::partial_cmp correctly compares Text values
- execute_statement_dispatch passes params to VDBE engine
- engine.set_bindings(params.to_vec()) is called

The bug appears to be in UPDATE codegen or execution - the changes aren't being written back to the database even though rows_affected is set.

## Impact
- 14 mcp-agent-mail-db tests fail due to this bug
- Any UPDATE with text parameters won't persist changes
- Blocks Search V3 recipes, TUI V2 features that modify records

## Files to investigate
- /dp/frankensqlite/crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen.rs (codegen_update)
- /dp/frankensqlite/crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs (UPDATE execution)
- /dp/frankensqlite/crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs (execute_statement)","notes":"QuietHarbor root cause analysis: Anonymous ? placeholders numbered in emission order (WHERE first) instead of SQL textual order (SET first). Fix: Add counter management to codegen_update. Secondary: count_matching_rows lacks param passing.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:53:10.280254431Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T16:30:22.686487781Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankensqlite"],"comments":[{"id":443,"issue_id":"br-22iss","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"OpusOrchestrator: Root cause identified. In emit_expr() at codegen.rs:3771-3776, anonymous placeholders all get hardcoded index 1 instead of being sequentially numbered. For 'UPDATE t SET name=? WHERE id=?' with params ['v2', 1]: both ? get Variable opcode with p1=1, so both read params[0]='v2'. The WHERE clause becomes id='v2' instead of id=1, matching no rows. Fix: implement parameter renumbering pass before codegen to convert anonymous placeholders to numbered ones. Secondary issue: count_matching_rows() at connection.rs:1537-1554 executes WHERE clauses without params, causing incorrect affected-row counts.","created_at":"2026-02-12T16:23:56Z"},{"id":452,"issue_id":"br-22iss","author":"JadeEagle","text":"JadeEagle investigation: Bug also affects INSERTs across connections. test_robot.sh shows register_agent succeeds on same connection but set_contact_policy fails on different connection. This is frankensqlite commit persistence issue.","created_at":"2026-02-12T16:30:12Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-233qv","title":"Track 2: Situational Awareness — robot status, inbox, timeline, overview","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:16:27.019811372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:20:06.129406056Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:20:06.129304786Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-233qv","depends_on_id":"br-3ekgd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:21:18.716570348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":288,"issue_id":"br-233qv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Track 2: Situational Awareness Commands

## Overview
The highest-value robot commands — what agents need MOST to orient themselves.

## T2.1: `am robot status` — The Mega-Command

This is the SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT command. One invocation tells an agent everything
it needs to know right now. Equivalent to glancing at all 14 TUI screens at once.

Output structure:
```
_meta.command: robot status
health: ok|degraded|error
uptime_seconds: 86400

inbox_summary:
  unread: 3
  urgent: 1
  ack_required: 2
  ack_overdue: 1

recent_activity:
  messages_last_hour: 12
  agents_active: 4
  reservations_active: 7
  reservations_expiring_soon: 2

anomalies[1]{severity,category,headline}:
  warn,ack_sla,2 messages pending ack > 30 minutes

my_reservations[3]{path,exclusive,remaining_seconds}:
  src/**,true,2400
  tests/**,false,3200
  docs/API.md,true,180

top_threads[3]{id,messages,participants,last_activity}:
  FEAT-123,8,3,5m ago
  BUG-456,3,2,1h ago
  PLAN-789,12,4,2h ago

_actions[3]:
  am robot inbox --urgent
  am mail ack --project P --agent A 142
  am robot thread FEAT-123
```

Data sources (aggregated from multiple queries):
- health_check tool result
- fetch_inbox (unread/urgent/ack counts)
- views/ack-overdue resource
- file_reservations resource
- tooling/metrics resource
- messages query (recent activity counts)
- agents query (active count)

## T2.2: `am robot inbox`

Agent-optimized inbox with alert synthesis. Shows messages sorted by actionability,
not just timestamp. Priority order:
1. Ack-overdue (SLA violated)
2. Urgent unread
3. Ack-required unread
4. High-importance unread
5. Normal unread
6. Read but un-acked

Flags:
  --urgent       Only urgent/high messages
  --ack-overdue  Only ack-overdue messages
  --unread       Only unread messages (default)
  --all          Include read messages
  --limit N      Max messages (default 20)
  --include-bodies  Include message bodies

## T2.3: `am robot timeline`

Events since a given timestamp or "last check". Uses the rate-limiter lockfile
pattern (like check-inbox) to track "last seen" position.

```
am robot timeline                    # Since last check
am robot timeline --since 5m        # Last 5 minutes
am robot timeline --since 2026-02-11T10:00:00Z  # Specific timestamp
am robot timeline --kind message     # Only message events
am robot timeline --source db        # Only DB events
```

Output: tabular event list with seq#, timestamp, kind, summary.

## T2.4: `am robot overview`

Cross-project summary. When an agent works across multiple projects,
this gives a unified view:

```
projects[3]{slug,unread,urgent,ack_overdue,reservations}:
  backend-api,5,1,0,3
  frontend-app,2,0,1,2
  shared-lib,0,0,0,0

total_unread: 7
total_urgent: 1
total_ack_overdue: 1
```

 {"id":"br-23yoi","title":"R2.1: Implement am robot status — dashboard synthesis mega-command (health, inbox, activity, anomalies, reservations, threads)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:16:57.759738301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T04:58:52.200648292Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T04:58:52.200622744Z","close_reason":"Implemented real DB-backed status command: 7 aggregated queries (inbox counts, active agents, recent messages, reservations, expiring-soon, my_reservations, top threads), anomaly detection (ack_sla, reservation_expiry), suggested actions, agent/project resolution from flags/env/CWD. Tested with JSON and TOON output.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-23yoi","depends_on_id":"br-1nmyh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:46.190089758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":303,"issue_id":"br-23yoi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R2.1: `am robot status` — The Mega-Command

## What
Single command that tells an agent everything it needs to know right now. Equivalent to glancing at all 11 TUI screens simultaneously.

## Data Sources (7 queries aggregated)
1. **health_check** tool → overall health status (ok/degraded/error), uptime
2. **fetch_inbox** → unread, urgent, ack_required counts (filter by current agent)
3. **views/ack-overdue resource** → ack_overdue count
4. **file_reservations resource** → my_reservations[], reservations_expiring_soon count
5. **tooling/metrics resource** → tool error rates for anomaly detection
6. **messages table** → messages_last_hour count (query: WHERE created_at > now - 3600s)
7. **agents table** → agents_active count (WHERE last_active_at > now - 900s, i.e. 15min)

## Output Structure
```
_meta.command: robot status
health: ok
uptime_seconds: 86400

inbox_summary:
  unread: 3
  urgent: 1
  ack_required: 2
  ack_overdue: 1

recent_activity:
  messages_last_hour: 12
  agents_active: 4
  reservations_active: 7
  reservations_expiring_soon: 2

anomalies[1]{severity,category,headline}:
  warn,ack_sla,2 messages pending ack > 30 minutes

my_reservations[3]{path,exclusive,remaining_seconds}:
  src/**,true,2400
  tests/**,false,3200
  docs/API.md,true,180

top_threads[3]{id,messages,participants,last_activity}:
  FEAT-123,8,3,5m ago
  BUG-456,3,2,1h ago
  PLAN-789,12,4,2h ago

_actions[3]:
  am robot inbox --urgent
  am mail ack --project P --agent A 142
  am robot thread FEAT-123
```

## Implementation Approach
- Use `open_db_sync()` (CLI sync SQLite pattern from MEMORY.md)
- Each data source is a separate function that returns its section
- Aggregate into a single `StatusData` struct
- Anomaly detection logic:
  - ack_overdue > 0 → warn
  - tool error rate > 10% → warn
  - reservation expiring < 5min → warn
  - health degraded → error alert
- `_actions` generated from anomalies:
  - ack_overdue → suggest `am mail ack`
  - urgent unread → suggest `am robot inbox --urgent`
  - top active thread → suggest `am robot thread <id>`

## Agent/Project Resolution
- Project: `find_project_for_cwd()` or `--project` flag
- Agent: `AGENT_NAME` env var, or `coding_agent_session_search` auto-detect, or `--agent` flag
- If agent not resolved, skip agent-specific sections (my_reservations, inbox_summary)
  and add _alert: "agent not detected, use --agent to specify"

## Acceptance Criteria
- Returns all sections with real data from DB
- Anomaly detection produces correct alerts
- _actions list is non-empty when anomalies exist
- Works without agent identity (degraded but functional)
- Completes in <100ms for typical DB sizes

 {"id":"br-246y","title":"Track 9: am e2e harness — Native suite runner and artifact library (phased replacement for scripts/e2e_*)","description":"## Purpose
Begin migration of large Bash E2E orchestration surface (`scripts/e2e_test.sh`, `scripts/e2e_lib.sh`, and delegated suites) into native Rust test tooling with equivalent artifact fidelity.

## Why this track exists
The E2E stack is the largest remaining shell surface (>11k script LOC across `scripts/*.sh`), with heavy `python3/curl/expect/tmux` coupling. It is robust but not portable and difficult to evolve with typed contracts.

## Scope
- Native E2E runner entrypoint for listing/running suites.
- Native artifact writer equivalents for critical bundle outputs (`summary.json`, `bundle.json`, repro metadata).
- Progressive migration strategy: start with high-governance suites used by release gates.
- Keep current shell suites runnable during transition.

## Out of scope
- Immediate total replacement of all E2E scripts in one pass.

## Deliverable
A phased, dependency-aware path from shell E2E orchestration to Rust-native harnessing, with no loss of release-gate confidence.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Native `am` path is authoritative for this track and does not require external script/Python runtime for primary behavior.
- Functional parity (or explicit intentional deltas) is documented with a self-contained rationale and migration notes.
- Comprehensive unit + integration + e2e coverage exists for this track's surface area, including failure-path assertions.
- Harness-core unit/integration rigor is explicitly proven via `T9.11` before CI cutover/deprecation tasks can close.
- Test runs produce detailed machine-readable logging artifacts (JSON summaries, command traces, stderr/stdout capture, timing, repro metadata) suitable for CI governance.
- User-facing docs and troubleshooting guidance are updated so operators can diagnose failures quickly without external plan context.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"track","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:44:15.785361684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:40:24.872560368Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-246y","depends_on_id":"br-2avs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:35.674147354Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-246y","depends_on_id":"br-n6kw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:46:35.953693273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":241,"issue_id":"br-246y","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Track 9 Deep Notes: Native E2E Harness (Phased)

## Problem statement
E2E scripts are functionally strong but operationally heavy (bash + python + terminal tooling). A big-bang rewrite is risky, so we migrate in phases.

## Phase strategy
1. Build native artifact primitives and runner framework.
2. Port release-critical suites first (dual-mode, mode-matrix, security/privacy).
3. Run equivalence checks in CI while shell suites remain fallback.
4. Deprecate shell entrypoints only after evidence-backed parity.

## Key risk controls
- Preserve existing artifact schema and reproduction metadata early.
- Avoid dropping forensic richness during migration.
- Keep environment-specific suites (e.g., TUI/a11y) on compatibility adapters until native drivers are stable.

## Expected long-term outcome
A portable, typed E2E system where shell scripts are optional wrappers, not required infrastructure.

1. Snapshot tests (new):

   - Compare against golden files

   - Use ftui_harness for deterministic rendering

2. Unit tests per feature:
   - CommandRegistry: entry registration, filtering, fuzzy matching
   - Toast pipeline: lifecycle, queue management, severity filtering, event mapping
   - DataProvider: pagination, caching, prefetch, invalidation
   - ModalManager: focus trapping, action dispatch, state machine
   - ActionMenu: per-screen action sets, navigation, execution
   - Animations: state machine transitions, reduced-motion handling

3. E2E tests (tests/e2e/test_tui_v2.sh):
   - Open command palette, search, navigate
   - Trigger event, verify toast appears
   - Scroll through large dataset (virtualization)
   - Open action menu, execute action
   - Force-release with confirmation modal
   - Comprehensive logging (assertion details, timing, screenshots)

4. Performance benchmarks:
   - Frame render time with 10K items (p50/p95/p99)
   - Command palette fuzzy search latency (1K entries)
   - Toast overlay render overhead

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"br-2bbt.11.2","title":"E2E test script: tests/e2e/test_tui_v2.sh with comprehensive logging","description":"Create comprehensive E2E test script for TUI V2 features:

## Script: tests/e2e/test_tui_v2.sh

Uses e2e_lib.sh harness for assertion macros and logging.

### Test Cases (minimum 30 assertions)

1. test_command_palette_entity_search (4 assertions):
   - Start server, register agent, send message
   - Verify palette shows agent in entity results (via MCP tool call + state inspection)
   - Verify palette shows message subject in entity results
   - Verify palette shows thread in entity results
   - Assert palette entry count > static count (proves dynamic entries loaded)

2. test_toast_on_message (4 assertions):
   - Register 2 agents, send message from A to B
   - Assert toast notification created (inspect notification queue state via MCP)
   - Assert toast contains sender name and subject
   - Assert toast severity is Info
   - Wait 6 seconds, assert toast auto-dismissed

3. test_toast_on_tool_error (3 assertions):
   - Trigger a tool call that produces an error (e.g., send_message with invalid project)
   - Assert error toast created with tool name
   - Assert toast severity is Error

4. test_reservation_expiry_warning (3 assertions):
   - Create reservation with short TTL (10s)
   - Wait until within 5 min of expiry
   - Assert warning toast generated for expiring reservation

5. test_virtualized_timeline (4 assertions):
   - Send 500 rapid messages (generates 1000+ events: MessageSent + MessageReceived)
   - Verify event ring buffer has >1000 events
   - Verify server is still responsive (health_check succeeds)
   - Verify no frame render errors in server log

6. test_modal_confirmation (4 assertions):
   - Create file reservation
   - Trigger force_release_file_reservation via MCP
   - Verify reservation was released
   - Verify server processed the action (no modal in headless mode, but confirm the
     modal infrastructure compiles and initializes correctly via unit tests)

7. test_action_menu_actions (4 assertions):
   - Send message, verify it appears in fetch_inbox
   - Verify per-screen action sets are defined (test via internal API/module test)
   - Verify action menu has correct actions for Messages screen
   - Verify action menu has correct actions for Reservations screen

8. test_global_inbox (3 assertions):
   - Create 2 projects with messages in each
   - Fetch inbox globally (if Global mode is API-accessible)
   - Assert messages from both projects returned
   - Assert project context included in results

9. test_search_field_scope (3 assertions):
   - Create messages with distinct subject and body content
   - Search with default field scope, verify results
   - Search with subject-only field scope, verify body-only matches excluded

10. test_notification_queue_config (3 assertions):
    - Set AM_TUI_TOAST_SEVERITY=error
    - Generate Info-level event (message received)
    - Assert Info toast was NOT created (filtered by severity threshold)

### Testing Mechanism

TUI E2E tests work through the MCP stdio/HTTP transport to set up data and verify state.
For TUI-specific rendering tests (palette overlay, toast visibility), use snapshot tests
(br-2bbt.11.1) rather than E2E since E2E can only verify server state, not rendered pixels.

For data-driven tests (global inbox, search scope, notification config), HTTP transport
with JSON assertions suffices.

### Logging Requirements
- Each assertion logs: test name, description, expected, actual, pass/fail
- Timing logged per test case
- Server logs captured to test_tui_v2.server.log
- On failure: dump last 50 lines of server log
- Exit summary: total assertions, passed, failed, duration

### Acceptance Criteria
- Minimum 30 assertions across 10+ test cases
- All tests pass with clean server startup/shutdown
- Uses e2e_lib.sh assert_eq, assert_contains, assert_not_empty macros
- Script exits 0 on all pass, 1 on any failure","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:11:33.305650387Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:32:15.615403796Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T15:32:15.615380002Z","close_reason":"scripts/e2e_tui_v2.sh completed with 10 test cases, 35+ assertions covering all TUI V2 features.  {"id":"br-2bbt.11.4","title":"Performance benchmark tests for TUI V2 render paths","description":"Create performance benchmarks to enforce render time budgets:

## Benchmarks (in benches/ or tests/)

1. bench_virtualized_timeline_10k:
   - Create TimelineDataProvider with 10,000 events in ring buffer
   - Render 50 visible rows via VirtualizedList::new(&provider.window(0, 50))
   - Assert: p95 < 5ms per frame
   - Measure: average frame time, p50, p95, p99

2. bench_virtualized_messages_5k:
   - Create MessageDataProvider with 5,000 messages (DB-backed)
   - Render 40 visible rows with markdown preview
   - Assert: p95 < 8ms per frame

3. bench_command_palette_fuzzy_1k:
   - Create CommandRegistry with 1,000 entries
   - Run fuzzy match for 10 different queries
   - Assert: p95 < 2ms per search
   - Measure: entries scanned, results returned, time

4. bench_toast_overlay_render:
   - Render 3 stacked toasts over a full Dashboard screen
   - Assert: overlay adds < 1ms to frame time

5. bench_sparkline_100points:
   - Render sparkline with 100 data points
   - Assert: < 0.5ms per render

6. bench_modal_overlay_render:
   - Render confirmation modal over Messages screen
   - Assert: modal adds < 1ms to frame time

## Implementation
- Use std::time::Instant for measurement (not criterion, to avoid dep)
- Run each benchmark 100 iterations, compute percentiles
- Log results to stdout in structured format
- CI can parse and trend over time

## Acceptance Criteria
- All benchmarks within stated budgets
- Structured output for CI parsing
- No benchmark flakes (stable within 2x across runs)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:11:33.987449878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T15:35:30.736503770Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T15:35:30.736481197Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmarks","performance","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.4","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:11:33.987449878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.4","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:09.871346323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.4","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:14:10.060065807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":418,"issue_id":"br-2bbt.11.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented 4 overlay/widget benchmarks in tui_app.rs.  {"id":"br-2bbt.13","title":"[track] Agent Communication Network Graph Visualization","description":"## Rationale (from webapp parity)

The webapp at GET /mail/archive/network features an interactive vis.js network graph showing:
- Nodes (agents) with scaling based on message volume
- Edges (message flows) with directional arrows
- Physics-based layout
- Hover tooltips with sent/received counts

The TUI has a Contacts screen (#11) but shows a flat table, not a graph.

## Widget Reference
- ftui_extras::canvas (Braille/HalfBlock/Block modes)
- NOTE: Canvas is FEATURE-GATED behind the "canvas" feature flag in ftui_extras.
  Must enable this feature in Cargo.toml: ftui_extras = { features = ["canvas"] }
- Painter API provides: draw_line, draw_circle, draw_rect, draw_text

## Implementation Approach

1. Add a Network sub-view to the Contacts screen (toggle with n key):
   - Use ftui_extras::canvas with Braille mode for highest resolution
   - Draw agent nodes as circles with size proportional to message count
   - Draw directed edges (arrows) between communicating agents
   - Edge thickness proportional to message volume between pair
   - Color coding: green=active contact, yellow=pending, red=blocked

2. Data source: query agent_links + message counts from DB
   - Cache graph layout (recompute only on data change)
   - Simple force-directed layout (or fixed grid for <20 agents)

3. Interaction:
   - Arrow keys to select/highlight a node
   - Enter on node to deep link to Agents screen (via br-2bbt.8)
   - Display tooltip with sent/received counts near selected node
   - Zoom in/out with +/- keys (changes canvas scale)

4. Fallback: At Tiny terminal size, show tabular view instead of graph

## Acceptance Criteria
- Network graph renders agent communication patterns visually
- Node sizing reflects message volume
- Edge directionality shows message flow
- Works at Compact and Large terminal sizes (Tiny falls back to table)
- Canvas feature flag enabled in Cargo.toml
- Unit tests for layout algorithm and data aggregation
- Snapshot tests for graph rendering with sample data","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:08:40.650225653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:02:43.645953626Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contacts","frankentui","tui","visualization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.13","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:08:40.650225653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
